ddls:
CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the project started */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the project ended */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the project (e.g., active, completed, on hold) */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team working on the project */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the project */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the model belongs to */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "algorithm" TEXT /* Algorithm used for the model */,
  "hyperparameters" TEXT /* Hyperparameters used for training the model */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the model was created */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset used for training the model */,
  "training_time" TEXT /* Time taken to train the model */,
  "training_accuracy" REAL /* Training accuracy of the model */,
  "cross_validation_score" REAL /* Cross-validation score of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_models_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_models_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the dataset belongs to */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the dataset */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the dataset was uploaded */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the dataset */,
  "data_size" REAL /* Size of the dataset in MB or GB */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of the dataset (e.g., tabular, image, text) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the dataset (e.g., internal, external, public) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "folds" (
  "fold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fold */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model the fold belongs to */,
  "fold_number" TEXT /* Number of the fold (e.g., fold_0, fold_1) */,
  "train_size" INTEGER /* Number of training samples in the fold */,
  "test_size" INTEGER /* Number of test samples in the fold */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the fold was created */,
  "cross_validation_method" TEXT /* Method used for cross-validation (e.g., K-Fold, Stratified K-Fold) */,
  "test_accuracy" REAL /* Test accuracy of the model on this fold */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_folds_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each set of metrics */,
  "fold_id" INTEGER /* ID of the fold the metrics belong to */,
  "mae" REAL /* Mean Absolute Error (MAE) */,
  "rmse" REAL /* Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) */,
  "mape" REAL /* Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) */,
  "max_error" REAL /* Maximum Error */,
  "f1_score" REAL /* F1 Score */,
  "precision" REAL /* Precision */,
  "recall" REAL /* Recall */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metrics_fold_id FOREIGN KEY ("fold_id") REFERENCES folds ("fold_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data scientist, model evaluator, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the project or model */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project being accessed */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the project or model was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the platform */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model version */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model the version belongs to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes in this version */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "training_time" TEXT /* Time taken to train the model for this version */,
  "training_accuracy" REAL /* Training accuracy of the model for this version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_versions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset version */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the version belongs to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes in this version */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "data_size" REAL /* Size of the dataset in MB or GB for this version */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of the dataset (e.g., tabular, image, text) for this version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_versions_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)
